alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; product ownership including requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout
alignments.job_requirement: Own technical delivery across multiple deployments from first prototype to stable production

alignments.candidate_evidence: Google Services role involved cross-functional stakeholder management and deployment experience; transformation program work requires customer/partner alignment
alignments.job_requirement: Embed closely with customer teams, understand their needs, and guide adoption of what you build

alignments.candidate_evidence: Candidate has no production software-engineering background; Python is basic and actively learning; product experience comes from ownership and requirements, not native engineering
alignments.job_requirement: Write and review production-grade code across frontend and backend using Python, JavaScript, or comparable stacks

alignments.candidate_evidence: No stated experience building or deploying LLM/generative AI systems; candidate positioned in Strategy and Operations, not AI-systems deployment
alignments.job_requirement: Have built or deployed systems powered by LLMs or generative models and understand how model behaviour affects product experience

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google in commercial operations and transformation, with deployment and rollout experience, but not in engineering or technical system deployment
alignments.job_requirement: Bring 5+ years of engineering or technical deployment experience that includes customer-facing work

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation programs and global operations scaling suggest experience with complexity and ambiguity, but in ops/strategy context rather than technical systems
alignments.job_requirement: Have scoped and delivered complex systems in fast-moving or ambiguous environments

gaps.gap: No production software-engineering background; candidate lacks hands-on coding experience in Python, JavaScript, or comparable stacks
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require significant ramp-up on full-stack development; could potentially pair with engineers, but FDE role explicitly requires direct code contribution

gaps.gap: No stated experience building or deploying LLM/generative AI systems; no model behavior or production AI inference understanding
gaps.mitigation: This is a frontier-model deployment role; lack of LLM systems experience is a material gap not easily closed in onboarding

gaps.gap: Technical deployment experience is in ops/commercial context, not in architecting or deploying complex software or AI systems
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's strength is in transformation and operations leadership, not technical systems architecture or production engineering

gaps.gap: Limited consulting or classic case-team experience; no indication of scoping and delivering bespoke technical solutions to external customers
gaps.mitigation: FDE role requires direct customer partnership and rapid technical scoping; this is candidate's weaker modality

output.level_rationale: Role requires 5+ years of engineering or technical deployment experience, customer-facing work, scoping and building complex technical systems, and production coding. Reporting to Model Deployment for Business suggests L5-L6 scope. Company size unknown but OpenAI's stage and customer-strategic nature signal senior IC or lead role.
output.summary: This Forward Deployed Engineer role is positioned in technical systems deployment and production engineering—a core-engineering family, not Strategy and Operations. Candidate brings eight years of proven ops/transformation leadership and customer-facing program delivery, but lacks production software-engineering skills, LLM/AI systems experience, and technical scoping expertise that are central to the FDE mandate. While seniority and complexity exposure align, the technical and AI-systems gaps are too large and domain-mismatched for the role.
uncertainties: OpenAI company stage and team size not stated in JD; inferred L5 based on customer-strategic scope
Candidate's Python 'basic and actively learning' level is ambiguous—unclear if this represents weeks of exposure or substantial partial mastery
No detail on candidate's exposure to AI/ML product context or research-to-production workflows at Google
FDE role emphasizes 'contribute directly in the code when progress or clarity depends on it'—unclear if this is aspirational or a hard operational requirement
